findings.bug_found: extractUid falsely extracted 6-digit hex substring from vd_source query params as a high-confidence UID. Video page URLs like https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV.../?vd_source=... contained 6+ digit hex sequences that passed the free-text fallback.
findings.root_cause: extractUid only rejected inputs STARTING with BV/AV, not URLs containing /video/BV in the path. The vd_source parameter is a 32-char hex string that frequently contains 6+ digit decimal sequences.
findings.fix: Added video-page URL rejection check (bilibili.com/video/, b23.tv/, bilibili.com/bangumi/) before the free-text extraction step. This catches video URLs before they hit the digit-sequence scanner.
enhancements: PowerShell link classification: added b23.tv detection, UID label parsing (uid:/mid:), 4+ digit UIDs, better logging
Updated run-bilibili-video.links.example.ps1 with all supported URL formats
Added 15 new test cases covering: URLs without https://, query params, 16-digit UIDs, video URL rejection

config.target: URL format handling in scraper input (extractUid, extractBvid, PowerShell link classification)
